title: Purged chronological CV with lap embargo around pit transitions
summary: This target is effectively a one-step-ahead event label, so contiguous laps from the same driver/race make leakage easy: lag features, expanding priors, and field aggregates can all see information from immediately adjacent future states.
rationale: This target is effectively a one-step-ahead event label, so contiguous laps from the same driver/race make leakage easy: lag features, expanding priors, and field aggregates can all see information from immediately adjacent future states. scikit-learn's TimeSeriesSplit supports an explicit gap, which is a direct way to test whether recent gains are real or boundary leakage.
implementation_hint: Sort by (Year,Race,Driver,LapNumber) or by race chronology, validate on future blocks or held-out races, and apply at least a 1-2 lap embargo between train and validation windows for each driver-race sequence. Recompute all encodings and historical priors inside each fold only. Keep only feature families that still help under the purged scheme.
expected_effect: May lower local CV but should improve trustworthiness and reduce over-optimizing to adjacent-lap leakage, giving a better chance of positive public-LB transfer.
risk: If the test distribution is iid rather than chronological, this can be overly harsh and discard useful signal. The main gain may be measurement quality rather than a direct score increase.
